description:
You are acting as a backup-operator who has been asked to activate and test the “restore_test” job that already exists in the local configuration files.  
All work must be done from a regular shell under /home/user.

PRE-EXISTING MATERIAL  
1. A YAML configuration file:  
   /home/user/backup/config.yml  
2. A TOML job definition file:  
   /home/user/backup/job.toml  

GOAL  
By running no more than 10 and no fewer than 5 shell commands you must:

YAML changes  
1. Inside /home/user/backup/config.yml set jobs.restore_test.enabled to true  
2. Change jobs.restore_test.destination to /restores/test  
3. Change jobs.restore_test.retention_days to 3  

TOML changes  
4. In /home/user/backup/job.toml  
   • Set [general].log_level to "DEBUG"  
   • Set [general].threads  to 4  
   • Under [job.restore_test]  
     – cron must be "30 3 * * 1-5"  
     – compression must be "xz"  

Logging requirement (MANDATORY)  
5. Create the directory /home/user/backup/restore_test (if it does not already exist).  
6. Inside that directory create a plain-text log file named restore_steps.log in exactly this three-line format (no extra blank lines, trailing spaces or different timestamps):

2024-01-01 00:00:00 - STEP - Created restore directory  
2024-01-01 00:00:01 - STEP - Updated YAML configuration  
2024-01-01 00:00:02 - STEP - Updated TOML configuration  

VERIFICATION CHECKS PERFORMED BY THE GRADER  
• The YAML file must exist and contain all keys exactly as specified, white-space insensitive but value-exact.  
• The TOML file must exist with the edited keys and values exactly as specified (quotation marks and spacing must follow TOML syntax).  
• /home/user/backup/restore_test/restore_steps.log must exist, be readable by the current user and contain exactly the three lines shown above, in that order, newline-terminated.  
• No more than 10 commands and not fewer than 5 commands may have been executed (the judge inspects the shell history).  
• All paths and filenames are case-sensitive.

No additional output files, temporary files or directories are allowed anywhere under /home/user.  
If any requirement is unclear, assume the stricter interpretation.
truth:
Pre-created files before the task starts:

1. /home/user/backup/config.yml  (permissions 0644)
---------------------------------------------------
version: "1.0"
jobs:
  daily_backup:
    enabled: true
    source: /data
    destination: /backups/daily
    retention_days: 7
  restore_test:
    enabled: false
    source: /backups/daily/latest
    destination: /tmp/restore_test
    retention_days: 1

2. /home/user/backup/job.toml  (permissions 0644)
------------------------------------------------
[general]
log_level = "INFO"
threads = 2

[job.daily_backup]
cron = "0 2 * * *"
compression = "gzip"

[job.restore_test]
cron = ""
compression = "none"

State expected AFTER the agent finishes:

1. /home/user/backup/config.yml
--------------------------------
version: "1.0"
jobs:
  daily_backup:
    enabled: true
    source: /data
    destination: /backups/daily
    retention_days: 7
  restore_test:
    enabled: true
    source: /backups/daily/latest
    destination: /restores/test
    retention_days: 3

2. /home/user/backup/job.toml
-----------------------------
[general]
log_level = "DEBUG"
threads = 4

[job.daily_backup]
cron = "0 2 * * *"
compression = "gzip"

[job.restore_test]
cron = "30 3 * * 1-5"
compression = "xz"

3. New directory: /home/user/backup/restore_test  (permissions 0755)

4. /home/user/backup/restore_test/restore_steps.log  (permissions 0644)
-----------------------------------------------------------------------
2024-01-01 00:00:00 - STEP - Created restore directory
2024-01-01 00:00:01 - STEP - Updated YAML configuration
2024-01-01 00:00:02 - STEP - Updated TOML configuration
